source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM AND HOW WE WORK:

Building the OS for the built world is no small feat. We’ll need to ship a LOT of high quality code in the coming years, and to do so, we’re building an engineering culture centered around craft, ownership, and collaboration. >25% of our engineers and product managers are ex-founders, and we hire for slope, not intercept. Being a place where you can just build things requires talent density and some unique ways of doing things.



A few ways we’re different:

 - No rules…rules: Many engineering teams design processes and rules around the lowest common denominator. Not us! We trust brilliant engineers to deliver magical customer outcomes and introduce processes and rules only when necessary.

 - Full-full-stack: Our technology stack (TypeScript everywhere!) and processes are designed to encourage engineers to run their projects end-to-end. This usually involves writing technical blueprints, implementing full-stack solutions, and talking to customers directly to get feedback.

 - Lean and AI-native: AI is turning 10x engineers into 100x engineers. We believe lean, talent-dense, AI-native teams will outship much larger teams with less talent density.

 - Blueprints and building blocks: One of our core Miter values is “build the blueprint”. Writing things down clarifies our thinking and aligns our teams. This also applies to building fundamental building blocks and abstractions that make developers’ lives easier.

 - We feel our customers’ pain and celebrate their success: We encourage engineers to build direct relationships with customers, feel their pain, and celebrate their success. We love fast feedback loops.

 - We’re meritocratic: We value results and hard work. We reward engineers who consistently ship and deliver for customers with increased scope and compensation.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Design, build, and maintain products/systems that power our core platform.

 - Collaborate with product managers and other engineers to deliver high-quality experiences.

 - Actively participate in blueprinting, code reviews, and technical documentation for your team.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 2+ years of professional software development experience.

 - Strong problem-solving skills.

 - A growth mindset and curiosity to understand business context and user needs.

 - Strong work ethic and reliability when owning tasks and deliverables.

 - A team-oriented attitude with humility and openness to new ideas.




OUR TECH STACK:

 - React + React Native

 - Node/Express

 - MongoDB

 - Render

 - Check

 - Typescript




OUR INTERVIEW PROCESS:

 - Recruiter Intro

 - A technical screen where we pair on a real-life pr

 - Improve technical quality on the team by giving detailed technical feedback on blueprints and in code review.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 4+ years of software engineering experience with a track record of delivering production systems.

 - Expertise in technical problem solving, data modeling and API development.

 - Curiosity to deeply understand business context and user needs.

 - Strong work ethic and reliability when owning tasks and deliverables.

 - Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex ideas in writing.

 - Collaborative nature with humility, empathy, and a drive to elevate those around you with thoughtful techn

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

Miter is helping contractors re-design their businesses, giving them tools to better manage the complex workflows and challenges they face in the back office and at the job site. We’re in the early days of our journey, and the potential to transform construction for the better is massive.

Design is AI first and builder-led at Miter. We leverage tools like Cursor and Claude to move quickly from idea to concept, explore flows and interactions, and then progress to Figma and Storybook for systems and polish. We stay close to the customer, ship quickly to learn, and embrace new ways of working.

As Staff Product Designer, you’ll play a critical role by leading design strategy and execution. You’ll meet with our customers to learn about their unique needs and workflows. You’ll collaborate daily with product and engineering, enabling the team to ship features that raise the bar on quality and user experience (and even shipping small features of your own). And you’ll play a key role shaping how we leverage AI to deliver consistent, delightful UX.



What You’ll Do:

 - Own design strategy and execution (interaction design, design system and componentry) across the Miter platform

 - Partner closely with peers in engineering and product (as well as cross-functional teams at Miter) to shape the user experience for both new and existing Miter apps

 - Work directly with our customers to learn how they use Miter and how it fits into the complex workflows that they use to run their business

 - Help shape the vision for AI capabilities at Miter and bring a user-centered lens to AI-powered functionality

 - Experiment with new AI tools, processes, and ways of working across product, design, and engineering

 - Operate with pragmatism and a first-principles approach to help the team deliver value to customers quickly and consistently




What You’ll Need:

 - 7+ years in product design at a high-growth software company

 - Strong user-centered design skills - you have a keen understanding user-centered design principles, usability, and accessibility standards, demonstrated through a strong portfolio of work

 - Strong leadership and collaboration skills - you have a track record of working effectively with product, engineering and cross-functional teams to define design requirement and align on goals

 - A bias for action and delivery - You’d rather get something valuable in front of customers from this week than perfect it for a month. You understand the benefit of shipping incremental value and optimizing for rapid learning

 - You are AI-native in how you work - you are eager to leverage AI tools to extend what and

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, App Founder
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.






About the Team & How We Work

Miter is building a suite of apps for contractors — and we need founders to build them. This is not a traditional PM role. As App Founder, you'll own a new app from zero: choosing the problem, finding the customers, building the prototype, and growing it into a $10M+ ARR business.



You'll work with a tiny team at first (often just you and another engineer), supported by Miter's platform, customer base, and GTM team. You'll report directly to the Head of Product and have visibility with the ELT. Think of it like being a seed-stage founder — with the distribution advantages of a scaling company behind you.



We move fast, operate with high trust, and expect high ownership. If you've ever wanted to build something from scratch without giving up the rocket ship you're already on, this is the role.



What You'll Do

 - Run deep customer discovery across 2–3 candidate problem areas and identify the most compelling app opportunity

 - Build a working prototype within your first 90 days — using AI coding tools, no-code platforms, or whatever it takes

 - Pitch and demo your app to real prospects, incorporate their feedback, and iterate rapidly to find product-market fit

 - Own the commercial motion: pricing, packaging, early sales, and customer expansion

 - Graduate the app through Miter's stage-gated investment framework (Discovery → Pre-Seed → Seed → Series A)



What You'll Need

 - 3+ years in product, founding, or full-stack engineering at a high-growth software company

 - A track record of launching 0→1 products — you've started something, shipped it, and learned from it

 - Comfort working with AI coding tools to build and ship prototypes independently

 - Sharp customer instincts — you can run great discovery interviews and know a real pain point when you hear one

 - High ownership mindset — you treat this like a business, not a project



Why This Role Is Different

Most PM jobs are feature factories. This one isn't. You'll have:

 - Founder-level ownership over a new product line

 - Significant milestone-based bonuses — $M++ in cash and equity across graduation stages

 - Direct access to Miter's customer base of thousands of contractors from day one

 - A clear path to becoming GM of a $10M+ ARR category inside a fast-growing company



Our Interview Process

 - Recruiter Screen

 - Hiring Manager Interview

 - Take-Home Assignment

 - Team Round Interviews


OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive (well above “market”) salary, commission, and equity packages.

 - Medical Insurance: Comprehensive medical, dental, FSA, vision plans to suit you and your family’s needs.

 -

source_jobs.title: Solutions Engineer
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.






About the Team & How We Work:

Solutions Engineers sit at the intersection of product, integrations, and customer operations and play a critical role in making sure our customers successfully launch and scale on Miter. You'll work closely with our Launch, Product, and Engineering teams to remove technical roadblocks, design integration strategies, and help customers navigate complex decisions across their business systems. You'll also flex into the sales process to support technical discovery and help our most complex prospects understand how Miter fits into their tech stack. This is a high-impact, highly visible role for someone who loves solving hard problems and building things that scale.


What You’ll Do:

 - Diagnose and resolve technical roadblocks during customer implementations — including configuration challenges, integration issues, and complex workflow design

 - Partner with Launch Managers, Product, and Engineering to scope technical requirements, evaluate feasibility, and implement lightweight solutions when possible

 - Support pre-sales by participating in technical discovery, evaluating integration requirements, and delivering tailored demos for complex prospects

 - Build and maintain internal documentation on integration patterns, product workflows, and recommended technical configurations to scale knowledge across the team

 - Develop repeatable approaches for common technical challenges so the broader team can move faster



What You’ll Need:

 - 2-3+ years in a customer-facing Solutions Engineer or technical implementation role

 - Experience working with APIs and system integrations, with the ability to analyze and design data flows across multiple platforms

 - Strong troubleshooting skills across technical workflows and configurations, including comfort reading and interpreting API specs and technical documentation

 - Ability to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Strong cross-functional collaborator — comfortable working across Product, Engineering, Sales, and Launch

 - Calm under pressure with a bias toward practical, scalable solutions and comfort navigating ambiguity

 - Experience with construction industry software, accounting systems, or ERP integrations (e.g. NetSuite, Sage, Viewpoint) is a plus



Our Interview Process:

 - Recruiter Screen

 - Hiring Manager Interview

 - Team Round Interview

 - Take Home Assignment + Presentation

 - Final Executive Interview


OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive (well above “market”) salary, commission, and equity packages.

 - Medical Insurance: Comprehensive medical, dental, F

source_jobs.title: Head of Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

Partnerships represents one of the most exciting and high-impact growth opportunities at Miter. Reporting directly to our Head of Sales, you'll work closely with Sales, Product, and Marketing to build and lead a partnerships function that becomes a durable, scalable revenue channel for the business.

This is a rare opportunity to architect something meaningful from the ground up — defining the strategy, expanding our partner ecosystem, supporting our existing partners, and building the infrastructure and processes that connect Miter's platform to the customers who will benefit most from it. You'll have direct visibility across the organization and a clear path to building and leading a team as the function scales. We're looking for a builder who is deeply customer-oriented, thrives in ambiguity, and wants to make a lasting impact in a $2T industry that is overdue for a technology transformation.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Build and scale Miter's partnerships function from scratch — covering program design, partner acquisition, relationship management, and channel infrastructure

 - Manage partner tiers, incentive structures, onboarding flows, and enablement programming

 - Coordinate co-sell motions with Sales, surface integration opportunities with Product, and support co-marketing with Marketing

 - Define and track core KPIs including partner-sourced pipeline, influenced ARR, activation rates, and goal attainment

 - Sign first partners, establish repeatable motions, and build the infrastructure that makes partnerships a durable revenue channel




WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - 8+ years of experience in partnerships, business development, or channel sales, with at least 3 years in a SaaS environment and a track record of building or scaling a partnerships function

 - A strategic background with experience building operational and business processes from the ground up

 - Comfort architecting channel strategy, conducting market analysis, and driving execution

 - Proficiency with CRM platforms, data analytics tools, and AI; ability to build operational infrastructure including deal registration, attribution tracking, and performance reporting

 - Excellent relationship-building skills — someone who earns trust from external partners and internal stakeholders equally

 - A bias for action, strong operator instincts, and comfort with ambiguity; you think in systems and build processes that scale beyond yourself

 - Clear, direct communication skills with the ability to represent Miter to senior partners and influence cross-functional teams

 - Experience in the construction industry, vertical SaaS, or workforce/pay

source_jobs.title: Launch Manager
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

As a Launch Manager at Miter, you’ll guide customers through onboarding from kickoff to go-live, owning implementations end-to-end—migrating data, configuring plans, training users, and clearing blockers along the way. You’ll keep timelines tight, communication clear, and stakeholders aligned to deliver a seamless experience.



You’ll partner with Sales, Product, Engineering, and Customer Success teams, gaining exposure across the company while directly shaping how quickly customers realize value. This is a hands-on, high-impact role where your work lays the foundation for scaling hundreds of launches as we grow.



Our Launch team is collaborative, detail-oriented, and energized by solving complex problems together. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, love ownership, and enjoy turning ambiguity into structure, you’ll fit right in. Plus, you’ll be part of a team that genuinely enjoys working together and making a tangible impact for our customers.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Proactively manage the full implementation process for your book of business, providing visibility into project status and risk

 - Partner with customers to understand their requirements, and consult on optimal Miter configuration for their needs

 - Migrate employee and payroll data from legacy systems into Miter, ensuring accuracy and completeness

 - Provide training and product guidance to employers and brokers throughout the implementation process

 - Troubleshoot and resolve implementation blockers quickly and calmly, maintaining trust with the customer

 - Collaborate closely with Product and Engineering to prioritize enhancements that will make launches smoother and faster
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 3+ years of SaaS implementation/onboarding, consulting or project management experience

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, able to simplify complex concepts for non-experts

 - Proven ability to manage multiple projects and stakeholders simultaneously

 - Customer-first mindset with high empathy and ownership

 - Strong problem-solving skills and ability to adapt when priorities shift

 - Detail-oriented with exceptional organizational skills




INTERVIEW PROCESS:

 - Recruiter Screen

 - Hiring Manager Interview

 - Take-Home Exercise

 - Mock Implementation Presentation

 - Leadership Interview




OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive (well above “market”) salary, commission, and equity packages.

 - Medical Insurance: Comprehensive medical, dental, FSA, vision plans to suit you and your family’s needs.

 - 401(k) Retirement Plan: Company-matched contributions to help you

source_jobs.title: Business Systems & Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK

You'll join a small, high-leverage team building the operating systems that power Miter from the inside. We believe that a better-built world starts with a better-built Miter, so we’re rethinking how internal teams work, what tools they use, and how AI can multiply what each person ships.



To start, you'll be embedded with our Customer Experience (CX) org, where fragmented information and manual processes slow down the people closest to our customers. You'll have wide latitude to pick the right problems, design the right systems, and partner with engineers to ship them. We move fast, write things down, and care more about outcomes than activity.



This role sits at the intersection of product, ops, and applied AI. You'll build systems and deploy models that become the connective tissue of how Miter operates.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own Miter's internal product roadmap. Look across the business, identify the highest-leverage opportunities to improve how we operate, and design the systems to capture them, in close partnership with engineering.

 - Embed deeply with CX. Use AI, data, and internal tooling to remove friction and speed up execution where fragmented information and manual processes slow teams down.

 - Own core internal systems and data infrastructure. Make sure customer context, key metrics, and workflows are connected across tools, and accessible to the people (and agents) who need them.

 - Drive cross-functional projects through influence. Translate ambiguity into clear PRDs, oversee engineers via indirect reporting lines, and earn the trust of the internal teams you serve.
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 4+ years in a highly strategic generalist role (management consulting, product management, BizOps, or similar), including experience taking products from 0 to 1.

 - A nose for impact. You can look across a business and spot where a unit of effort returns an order of magnitude in value.

 - Baseline technical fluency. You're comfortable reading API docs, drafting PRDs, and stitching together internal tooling (including AI-powered workflows) without waiting on engineering for every step.

 - Clear linear thinking and writing. You can take large amounts of messy, imperfect information and turn it into a logical plan that others can act on.

 - Empathy and strong listening skills. You can hear the "so what" behind the pain your internal customers articulate, and translate it into the right thing to build.




OUR INTERVIEW PROCESS

 - Recruiter screen

 - Hiring manager interview

 - Presentation Round

 - Team Round Interviews




OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive Compensation: We offer competiti

source_jobs.title: Tax Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

As a Tax Operations Specialist, you’ll be Miter’s internal payroll tax expert - the person who turns tangled tax situations into clear, calm resolutions for our customers. Payroll tax is one of the highest-stakes parts of running payroll, and getting it right is core to the trust our customers place in Miter.

You’ll work closely with Support, Launch, Product, and our partners at Check to own reactive tickets, drive proactive risk reviews, build the playbooks and enablement that scale tax expertise across the company. We’re a small, scrappy team that prizes ownership, clear writing, and the instinct to fix the root cause, not just the symptom. If you love untangling messy problems, partnering with customers in stressful moments, and turning hard-won knowledge into systems that compound, this is your seat.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Serve as Miter’s internal tax expert: stay current on Check policy and product changes, federal/state/local filing rules, and agency nuances; be the go-to for teammates escalating complex cases.

 - Own reactive tax support tickets: tax notices, blocked filings, W-2/year-end issues, balancing/HLD debits, and general tax-setup questions.

 - Drive proactive tax setup reviews for customers flagged as high-risk during onboarding — benefit/deduction design, payroll history reconciliation, tax profile audits (agency IDs, TPA/POA, “Applied For,” frequencies, rates), First Quarter Filing readiness, and year-end/ownership-change prevention.

 - Build playbooks, enablement docs, and training for Sales, Launch, and the broader Support team so common tax issues can be handled systematically (and eventually automated).




WHAT YOU’LL NEED:

 - 3+ years (mid) / 5+ years (senior) of payroll tax experience at a payroll provider, payroll SaaS, or PEO, with hands-on knowledge of federal, state, and local filing requirements across multiple jurisdictions.

 - Direct experience owning agency notices end-to-end, including researching guidance, guiding customers through resolution, and handling cases from first email through live calls with stressed CFOs.

 - Strong Excel skills (pivot tables, lookups, gross/net and tax reconciliations) and working knowledge of payroll systems like Check, Gusto, Rippling, ADP, or Paychex, with the ability to build and interpret balancing reports from scratch.

 - Deep agency fluency: POA/TPA processes, "Applied For" handling, filing frequencies, wage bases, withholding mechanics, and triaging 941/940, state UI, withholding, and local notices into clear next steps.

 - Excellent written and verbal communication paired with strong judgment: can distill a tangled tax hi

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

As an Account Executive at Miter, you will own a defined geographic territory and be responsible for building it from the ground up. This is a full-cycle, field-oriented sales role where you will act as a consultative partner to contractors, guiding them to a better way of running their business. You will drive new account acquisition across your territory from initial outreach to close, developing deep local market knowledge and becoming a trusted advisor in your regional construction community. You will have a direct impact on our customers' ability to manage their teams, projects, and financials with greater efficiency, ultimately helping them build a better built world.

This role involves approximately 75% travel (12–15 working days per month). Most travel will be within your local territory and focused on customer meetings, events, and site visits. Travel expectations will be set and agreed upon in advance, with the majority being local or regional rather than cross-country.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Prospect & Qualify: Strategically manage geographically aligned prospects. Effectively target and qualify their “fit” based on Miter’s value proposition.

 - Consultative Selling: Conduct deep discovery to understand a contractor's unique business needs and pain points related to payroll, HR, and field operations. Be an expert on industry trends to establish credibility with C-suite executives looking to stay ahead.

 - Demonstrate Value: Effectively position and demonstrate how Miter's all-in-one platform provides contractors with a consolidated way to integrate all key components of running their business.

 - Drive Business: Negotiate and close new logo opportunities, consistently meeting and exceeding monthly and quarterly revenue targets.

 - Collaborate: Partner with internal teams, including sales development, launch and customer success, to ensure a seamless handoff and successful implementation for new customers.

 - Manage Pipeline: Maintain an organized and accurate forecast. Including up to date pipeline stages and forecast categories.

 - Be a Founder: Bring an entrepreneurial mindset to the team, identifying opportunities for improvement and contributing to a dynamic, rapidly growing company.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED:

 - 2+ years of quota-carrying, full-cycle B2B software sales experience with a track record of top performance

 - Demonstrated experience building and owning a territory: you know what it takes to generate pipeline from scratch and stay disciplined on the same accounts over time.

 - A founder mindset: you take ownership, don't make excuses, and treat your territory like a busine

source_jobs.title: Strategic Finance Generalist
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK

 

Finance at Miter is small, fast, and high-leverage. We’re the analytical engine behind the company’s biggest decisions – from how we invest in growth to how we run the day-to-day. As our Finance Generalist, you’ll work directly with the Head of Finance and partner with leaders across the company to build the budgets, models, and operating systems that help Miter scale with discipline.

 

This is a rare opportunity to wear many hats: one day you’re building a hiring-plan model, the next you’re automating a vendor workflow or building a self-serve dashboard for a functional leader. We move quickly, write things down, and care deeply about leaving every process better than we found it. If you love turning ambiguity into structure and want to see the direct impact of your work on a fast-growing business, this is the seat for you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Support financial planning and business visibility. Build and improve the budgets, forecasts, and operating models that support company decision-making. Partner closely with functional leaders to track KPIs, understand performance, and turn data into clear recommendations and actionable insights.

 - Build operational rigor into the finance function. Help run the financial engine of the company by managing procurement and vendor workflows, partnering with outsourced accounting, supporting month-end close processes, and improving the systems and processes that keep finance operating smoothly and efficiently.

 - Be a high-leverage analytical partner. Tackle a wide range of business problems – from evaluating hiring plans to analyzing marketing efficiency to building dashboards and self-serve reporting. Context-switching is part of the job. You’ll work closely with the Head of Finance to help leaders make better decisions through structured analysis and operational insight.

 - Automate and improve how the company operates. Leverage AI, automation, and better systems design to eliminate manual work and improve operational efficiency. We’re looking for someone who instinctively looks for ways to build leverage, streamline workflows, and leave every process better than they found it.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 2–6 years of experience in strategic finance, business operations, consulting, banking, analytics, or operating roles at a high-growth company.

 - Experience solving ambiguous business problems using structured thinking, strong business judgment, and data-driven analysis.

 - Track record of working cross-functionally to improve processes, build scalable workflows, and support better decision-making.

 - Strong financial modeling skills.

 - Comfort working

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative, Outbound
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

Our SDR team is the engine behind Miter’s pipeline growth — and we’re scaling fast. As an Outbound SDR, you’ll own prospecting within a newly established geographic territory model, partnering closely with Account Executives to execute a targeted, scalable outbound strategy across the construction industry.

This is a builder’s seat. You’ll have room to get creative as we shape a repeatable SDR playbook and a high-impact, data-driven sales engine, working alongside sales, marketing, and leadership to test new ideas, sharpen messaging, and accelerate revenue growth.

As the first point of contact for many of our future customers, you’ll set the tone for strong, lasting relationships and help contractors see the value of modern, industry-leading tools. And we’re committed to your growth: SDRs at Miter have a clear path into senior SDR roles, Account Executive positions, and beyond.



What You’ll Do:

 - Drive net-new pipeline through high-volume outbound prospecting — 80–100 dials a day across cold calls, personalized emails, and LinkedIn outreach

 - Research target accounts and key personas across the construction industry to craft relevant, tailored messaging that breaks into hard-to-reach accounts

 - Communicate Miter’s value proposition and business impact, qualifying prospects and booking ~5 direct SALs per month for your aligned Account Executive

 - Own the nurture of every prospect all the way through to a completed discovery call and a clean handoff to your AE

 - Own your activity and pipeline metrics in Salesforce, and help build a repeatable playbook by testing and refining messaging, cadences, and scripts as we grow the team



What You’ll Need:

 - No prior experience required — prior SDR work, cold calling, or outbound prospecting is a plus, along with athletic, competitive, or leadership experience

 - Strong written and verbal communication, with the confidence to pick up the phone and make a strong first impression with cold prospects

 - Grit, resilience, and a strong work ethic — you bounce back quickly from a “no” and bring consistent energy every day

 - A natural relationship builder who partners closely with AEs, asks sharp questions, and gets creative about breaking into target accounts

 - Self-motivated and coachable, with a genuine desire to build a long-term career in Sales

 - A true team player above all — you win as a team and lift up the people around you
   
   

Our Interview Process:

 - Recruiter Screen

 - Hiring Manager Interview

 - Take Home Assignment

 - Final Executive Interview
   
   


OUR BENEFITS:

 - Competitive Compensation: We offer competitive (well ab

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative, Inbound
source_jobs.description:
A BETTER BUILT WORLD https://www.miter.com/resources/a-better-built-world/

At Miter, we’re on a mission to help construction contractors build with confidence. If we’re successful, we’ll make it easier and faster to build critical physical infrastructure - roads, bridges, utilities, data centers, housing, etc.



For decades, construction and field services contractors have had to run their businesses on outdated software: clunky, on-premise systems created in the 1980s and 1990s.



That’s where Miter comes in. We’re using AI and embedded payments to rebuild the core HR, finance, and operations systems that underpin our physical economy. Using Miter, contractors like Marathon Electrical https://www.miter.com/customer-story/marathon-electrical-contractors/, W.J. O’Neil https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GNExtmeHcSg, and Truebeck Construction http://truebeck.com/ are building stronger teams, controlling job costs, and accelerating jobsite execution.



This idea is resonating. Since we launched in 2021, we’ve grown to thousands of customers and tens of millions in ARR, making us one of the fastest growing vertical software companies ever. To double-down on our momentum, we’ve raised $50M+ from top investors (Bessemer, Coatue, and Battery) who share our belief that we’re just getting started.







ABOUT THE TEAM & HOW WE WORK:

Our SDR team is the engine behind Miter’s pipeline growth — and we’re scaling it fast. As an Inbound SDR, you’ll be the first to connect with contractors who raise their hand to learn more about Miter, responding fast and turning that interest into qualified pipeline. You’ll work hand in hand with Marketing and Account Executives to make sure no opportunity slips through the cracks.

This is a high-impact seat with plenty of room to get creative. You’ll help sharpen our qualifying and follow-up motions, tie prospect pain to the right Miter modules, and shape a repeatable inbound playbook as the product evolves — working alongside sales, marketing, and leadership to test new ideas and accelerate revenue growth.

As the first point of contact for many of our future customers, you’ll set the tone for strong, lasting relationships and help contractors see the value of modern, industry-leading tools. We’re committed to your growth: SDRs at Miter have a clear path into senior SDR roles, Account Executive positions, and beyond.




What You’ll Do:

 - Be the first point of contact for inbound leads — demo requests, content downloads, and marketing-sourced leads — responding with a time-to-touch under 15 minutes

 - Run qualifying and discovery conversations to surface prospect pain and tie it back to the relevant Miter modules

 - Communicate Miter’s value proposition and business impact, booking qualified meetings (SALs) for Account Executives and managing clean handoffs

 - Follow up persistently with every lead that no-shows or doesn’t self-book, re-engaging across phone, email, and other channels

 - Partner closely with Marketing on campaigns and lead quality, and own your activity and pipeline metrics in Salesforce



What You’ll Need:

 - No prior experience required — prior SDR work, cold calling, or transferable experience from college (athletics, leadership, part-time sales) is a plus

 - Highly organized — you can juggle a high volume of inbound leads and follow-ups, staying on top of every prospect without letting anything slip through the cracks

 - Strong written and verbal communication — as the first point of contact at Miter, you’re energetic, personable, and make a great first impression

 - A natural relationship builder who partners closely with AEs, asks sharp questions, and stays genuinely curious about every prospect

 - Self-motivated and coachable, with a strong desire to build a long-term career in Sales

 - Grit, resilience, and a strong work ethic — you bring consistent energy and bounce back quickly from a “no”

 - A true team player above all — you win as a team and lift up the peop

notes: Miter is a construction tech company using AI and embedded payments to rebuild core HR, finance, and operations systems.
